text: The surgeon begins dissection at the medial umbilical ligament, working from the supravesicular space medially to laterally. The dissection progresses with visible adjustments to stay superficial in the supravesicular plane, pushing fat laterally towards Bogros space. The peritoneal flap is manipulated, and the surgeon reorients to maintain a thin flap.

The surgeon performs lateral dissection close to the peritoneal flap for an indirect inguinal hernia repair, ensuring the mesh will lay flat when closing the pocket. The dissection extends from the supravesicular space to the deep internal ring, identifying Cooper's ligament and the hernia sac at the neck of the hernia. The surgeon uses gentle cautery and sweeping motions, maintaining proximity to the flap while switching to an oblique angle.

The surgeon carefully pulls on the hernia sac to reveal the spermatic cord and vas deferens. Using gentle electrocautery, the surgeon sweeps the hernia sac down off the spermatic cord, allowing the vas deferens to come into view. The dissection continues with the surgeon working laterally to safely identify and separate the spermatic cord from the surrounding tissue.

The surgeon identifies the main vein branch off the chronomortis and places a medial stitch on Cooper's ligament, just medial to the vein. A deep pocket is created for an extra-large mesh, with visible anatomical landmarks including the vein to the left and iliacs to the right. The surgical field is cleared to ensure no evidence of recurrent direct space hernia.

The surgeon performs a high medial fixation on a right indirect inguinal hernia using a running canal baseball stitch. The stitch is applied with a two-oh V-lock, which becomes almost entirely absorbed and buried within the tissue as the procedure progresses.

The surgeon placates the indirect angle hernia sac, suturing it back onto the flap to prevent recurrence. The sac is swept up and sutured laterally, ensuring there are no holes or defects. The hernia sac is draped over the suture line, extending laterally.
